AI-Hydro

Interactive Module Contract Fixture

Deterministic runtime and course test fixture

1 · State and controls

Close a one-step water balance

The calculation uses equivalent water depths over one watershed and one common time step: ending storage = beginning storage + precipitation − evapotranspiration − runoff.

Expected result With the authored default beginning storage of 100 mm, the ending storage is 110 mm.
100 mm
python Create deterministic kernel state 
storage = {{storage}}
precipitation = 20.0
evapotranspiration = 4.0
runoff = 6.0
storage_next = storage + precipitation - evapotranspiration - runoff
print(f"ending_storage={storage_next:.1f} mm")
storage_next
2 · Shared namespace and image

Read prior state and render a figure

This cell intentionally reads storage_next from the first cell to test persistent state within one artifact.

python Read state and return a matplotlib figure 
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

labels = ["Initial storage", "Ending storage"]
values = [storage, storage_next]
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(5, 3))
ax.bar(labels, values)
ax.set_ylabel("Equivalent water depth (mm)")
ax.set_title("Deterministic water-balance state")
fig.tight_layout()
fig
3 · Error recovery

Render a deliberate error without crashing the panel

The error is intentional. Run All should continue to completion after the error result is returned.

python Deterministic error 
raise ValueError("intentional runtime-contract fixture error")
4 · Self-check

Complete the deterministic checkpoint

Using the authored defaults, what is the ending storage?

Inputs were subtracted from storage instead of added.
Both losses must be subtracted from the initial storage plus precipitation.

This is an inspectable self-check fixture, not a secure graded assessment. The current answer index is present in the HTML contract.

References

Mass closure is used here as an internal-consistency check, not as proof that process partitioning is physically correct.

Purpose
Runtime, kernel, state, output, quiz, progress, and navigation contract testing.
